title: Lasiosiphon passargei
intro: Lasiosiphon passargei, also known as the African "stonebreaker," is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae, traditionally used in African folk medicine. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Lasiosiphon passargei include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lasiosiphon passargei is often prepared as a tea or decoction, or used as a raw material for the production of various herbal supplements and capsules.
